description: Leiden community detection (modularity, 10 iterations, seed 42) on the undirected largest connected component, then label each cluster by its dominant top-level Mathlib module (pure if dominant module >=50%; mixed otherwise). Labeling: src/label_clusters.py.
motivation: If unsupervised clusters map to named modules, the domain structure of mathematics is latent in proof dependencies.
formal_results: 138 clusters over ~92,594 clustered theorems; 121 pure / 17 mixed; largest pure CategoryTheory 8,245 (54%). Concurs with Li et al. 2026 (Louvain, NMI=0.34).
results_description: Unsupervised clustering recovers Mathlib's module structure: most clusters are dominated by a single top-level module.
conclusions: Mathematical domains are recoverable from the proof graph with no labels.

comments.caveat: README's 'top clusters by size' table uses a different convention than the paper (e.g. README C0 ~12,513 'Algebra+Analysis+Data' vs paper's largest pure CategoryTheory 8,245); divergent numbers for the same objects.
